summary: Single-page React 19 + TypeScript app, bundled by Vite 6, styled with Tailwind 3, made installable by vite-plugin-pwa (Workbox autoUpdate). Curriculum is a typed data fixture compiled into the bundle. Live AI calls go directly from the browser to api.anthropic.com using the BYO API key in localStorage and the documented direct-browser-access header. No backend exists; this is the architectural guarantee that satisfies R-06 and R-07.

decisions.rationale: Mature SPA tooling; vite-plugin-pwa is first-class; reduces deploy complexity to a static dist/ directory which serves identically on GitHub Pages and S3.

decisions.decision: BYO Anthropic API key, stored only in localStorage; called directly from browser with `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true`
decisions.rationale: Eliminates the server, the shared-secret risk, and any cost the operator absorbs. The 'dangerous' header is appropriate here because the trust boundary is the user's own device and the user holds their own key.
tradeoffs: User must obtain and paste a key (mitigated by simulated offline mode, R-13).
Key sits in plaintext localStorage on her device (acceptable: same trust level as a browser-stored password; documented in README; one-click Forget Key).

decisions.decision: localStorage with a single versioned key `helm.v1.state`
decisions.rationale: Curriculum progress, key, model preferences fit in <10 KB; no need for IndexedDB.

decisions.decision: Tailwind 3 + custom token palette (ink/paper/gold/moss/rust) + Inter + Fraunces + JetBrains Mono
decisions.rationale: Banking-confident aesthetic per R-15; serif display gives editorial gravitas (Mercury/Brex reference); deep navy + warm paper + gold accent reads as fintech, not terminal.
HashRouter sidesteps GitHub Pages subpath-rewrite pain and works identically on S3 without CloudFront rewrite rules; trades clean URLs for deploy simplicity.

decisions.decision: vite-plugin-pwa with registerType=autoUpdate and Workbox precaching of all built assets
decisions.rationale: Satisfies R-10 (installable) and R-11 (offline) without a custom service worker.

decisions.decision: Two parallel deploys: GitHub Pages (gh-pages branch via gh-actions) + AWS S3 commercial bucket with public-read static-site hosting
decisions.rationale: GitHub Pages gives a friendly URL under github.io; S3 gives a partition-independent canonical URL operator controls. CloudFront is optional (added only if a custom domain is layered later).

decisions.decision: Claude Opus 4.7 (claude-opus-4-7), Sonnet 4.6 (claude-sonnet-4-6), Haiku 4.5 (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001); default = Haiku
decisions.rationale: Per global CLAUDE.md: latest family + sensible default. Haiku keeps exercise latency low and cost minimal for novice exploration.
Two knobs cover the sampling-curriculum teaching surface without overwhelming a novice; both are sent on every request.

non_functional.performance: First contentful paint < 1.5s on cold cache over 4G; offline navigation < 200ms.
non_functional.security: No backend, no analytics, no telemetry. Key is read-once per request from localStorage. No third-party scripts beyond Google Fonts CSS (CSS only, no JS).
